page: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture, consisting of 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the top 10% (3680.45), while multi-core performance ranks in the top 25% (86468.87). Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both achieve top-tier status, processing at 41.21 GB/sec and 17.71 GB/sec respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong cached read-only speed of 4950.45 GB/sec and a strong large-block write speed of 94.13 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read bandwidth drops to an average 157.02 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48ads_v7 offers a high-density resource profile with a balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and substantial bundled local storage. The inclusion of 2835 GB of local disk space reduces the immediate need for external block storage for temporary data, though the lack of complimentary public IPv4 addresses represents a minor networking tradeoff. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, relational databases, data compression tasks, and multi-threaded application servers.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 24 physical cores with multithreading and includes 2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-core and cached memory bandwidth results. Uncached large-block read bandwidth remains average at 157.02 GB/sec. With a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core and substantial local storage, this instance provides a resource-dense profile for general-purpose workloads, databases, and multi-threaded applications without the need for dedicated GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring forty-eight AMD EPYC vCPUs, substantial local storage, and top-tier single-core processing performance.

2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Strong multi-core processing and cached memory bandwidth.
Suited for general-purpose applications, databases, and compression tasks.
